title: Innerbloom 2.0 Marketing MVP - English Test
goal: Validate the full acquisition loop with two simple posts before planning a monthly 20-post system.

posts.hypothesis: People who have failed with streak-based apps will respond to "adaptive rhythm" and "real weeks".
caption: Your habits should adapt to your real life.
Most habit apps assume every day is the same.
Then a busy week hits, your streak breaks, and the whole plan starts feeling useless.
Innerbloom is built around adaptive rhythm:
- lower the intensity when life gets heavy
- keep visible progress
- recalibrate instead of starting over
- build a Journey that can survive real weeks
Early version is live.
Try it here:
{{trackingUrl}}
#habits #habitbuilding #selfimprovement #productivity #personalgrowth #wellbeing #buildinpublic

assets.title: Your habits should adapt to your real life.
assets.body: Adaptive habits for real weeks, not perfect calendars.

assets.title: Most habit apps assume every day is the same.
assets.body: Life does not. Stress, sleep, travel, work, and energy change constantly.

assets.title: Lower the intensity. Keep the direction.
assets.body: Innerbloom helps you recalibrate instead of restarting from zero.

assets.title: Build a Journey that can survive real weeks.

posts.hypothesis: A direct anti-perfect-days message will perform better for people tired of rigid productivity systems.
caption: If your plan only works on perfect days, it is not a plan.
Most people do not fail habits because they are lazy.
They fail because the system expects the same output from them every day, even when their energy, stress, sleep, and schedule change.
Innerbloom is an adaptive habit app.
It helps you keep direction without forcing the same rhythm all the time.
Start small. Recalibrate. Keep moving.
Try it here:
{{trackingUrl}}
#habits #selfimprovement #wellbeing #productivity #mentalclarity #habittracker #buildinpublic

assets.title: If your plan only works on perfect days, it is not a plan.
assets.body: Innerbloom adapts habit intensity to real life, so progress does not collapse when the week gets messy.
